summary: Gruber reviews the Richard Solo Backup Battery for iPhone 3G, motivated by his biggest complaint about the device: battery life on heavy-use days. He explains that while the iPhone's battery is adequate most days, it falls short when he 'goes commando' — leaving his laptop behind and relying solely on the iPhone. The $50 dongle-style battery pack adds about 50-60% charge and is pocket-friendly when not in use. He chose it over the Mophie Juice Pack case design for its portability, lower price, and cross-device compatibility. Despite minor complaints about a flimsy cap and confusing LEDs, he considers it a worthwhile solution for extending iPhone battery life through a heavy day.
key_points: The iPhone's battery problem stems not from poor engineering but from the device doing more than other phones, pushing battery technology limits
Gruber would pay more for a thicker, heavier iPhone with better battery life, though he understands why Apple maintains a single form factor
The dongle form factor beats the case form factor for portability, price, and cross-device compatibility
The battery provides roughly 50-60% charge restoration, enough to survive a full day of heavy iPhone-only use
The biggest design flaw is a cheap snap-on cap for the dock connector that falls off easily — a retractable connector would be better
money_quotes: The problem is that the iPhone pushes the limits of modern battery technology — what makes the iPhone great is that it can do so much, but doing that much consumes significant power.
I would actually pay more for an iPhone that was thicker and heavier but provided longer battery life.
The problem strikes on the days when I go commando — foregoing my MacBook Pro and using my iPhone as my sole connection to the Internet for the day.
I no longer lug my laptop with me when attending a conference, for example.
It somehow seems wrong to have the naked dock connector bouncing around in my pocket.
A better design would be for the dock connector to be retractable, sort of like the blade on a box cutter.
The bottom line, though, is that it's the next best thing to having an iPhone with a longer-lasting internal battery.
key_insight: The iPhone's battery challenge is a direct consequence of its ambition — it does so much that no battery can keep up on heavy-use days, making external battery packs a necessary compromise.
